iliiSi"i;, "?;,0"'o'
Fair PoIitical 
Practices Commission -- Legal Section
428 J St,, Suite 8OO
Sacramento, Ca. 
9 5814
Dear Sirs:
At the suggestion of one of your attorneys (John Wallace), I
am requesting expedited fornal written advice concerninE a slate
mailer which is under consideration for this November's election.
we are considering one or several nass nailinEs which would
feature joint 
recommendations by four candidates or officeholders:
one federal , two state, and one local officeholder, 
The local
officeholder 
is not up for reelectionl 
the other officeholders 
are
candidates this November. The nailings will go to voters in a
Iarge region, including but extending well beyond any one
officeholder's 
own district. 
The nailing will be organized and
produced by a consultinE group, at the behest of the recommending
officeholders, 
and the consultants will earn a profit 
through this
activity. 
These four officeholders or candidates will 
make
reconmendations concerning statewide partisan races, state
leEislative 
races, possibly Iocal races, and statewide and local
ballot measures. None of the four officeholders control comrnittees
for any of the ballot measures on which reconmendations will 
be
made. At Ieast four state candidates will be included among those
candidates or issues recomnended.
Our prinary concern centers around the potential for in-kind
contributions by the slate nailing to either those candidates
naking recommendations or those candidates being recommended. I an
pleased that the Commission seems to be noving toward the "express
advocacy" standard. 
To me, that approach makes the nost sense.
Therefore, I would Iike to outline the three alternative 
financing
arrangements we are considering and how we plan to interpret
Commission regulations. 
Since we nay move quickly to implement
these plans, we would appreciate an expedited response if the
Commission believes there night be significant 
problems with our
interpretation.
Our first 
approach would be to have some or all of those
candidates or measures being recommended (the recomnendees ) pay for
the nailing. 
Their shares would be calculated through a
combination of physical space and prorninence on the mailing, and
the size of their districts. 
Those officeholders featured on the
card as naking these recommendations (the reconmendors ) would not
be charged. (However, some recommendors may also be reconnendees,
in which case they would pay a fair share, calculated in the same
way aa for alI other recommendees. ) Those recommendees paying for
the mailing would be appropriately identified 
on the nailing.


Under this approach, we believe no in-kind contribution 
would
accrue to the recommendors. Nor would any in-kind contribution
accrue to any of reconmendees; those who are paying for the nailing
would be appropriately identified, 
and those who are not may still
be included on the slate maili.ng at the discretion of the slate
committee, given the size of the slate.
A second approach would be to have the recomnendors pay the
full cost of the nailing. 
In this caae aa well, we believe no
in-kind contribution would accrue to any recommendees, based on the
same slate mailer exemption.
Finally, 
a combination of the above two approaches may be
used, with both recommendors and recommendees provi.ding funding.
Once again, we believe no in-kind contribution would accrue to
either reconmendors or reconnendeea.
I hope the abowe infornation is sufficient to allow you to
nake an inforned judEnent about our interpretation of the current
FPPC regulations. I look forward to hearing from your office
shortly. 
If you need any additional infornation, please feel free
to caII me at home at (818) 449-7555 or at work at (213) 414-6915.
Thank you for your assigtance.
fll&**,
Fuhrman

sections.question: Are there potential in-kind contributions by a slate mailer to candidates making recommendations or candidates being recommended, and which of three proposed financing arrangements would comply with FPPC regulations?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks expedited formal written advice on whether three proposed slate mailer financing arrangements (payment by recommendees, payment by recommendors, or a combination) would violate in-kind contribution rules under FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal question/conclusion sections with headers are present. The legal question and proposed financing arrangements are embedded in the letter narrative. The document contains specific financing proposals (three alternatives) rather than seeking general guidance. Extraction confidence reduced slightly due to lack of formal Q/C structure and some document OCR quality issues in the letterhead.

embedding.summary: A consultant group requests expedited FPPC advice on three financing arrangements for a multi-candidate slate mailer featuring joint recommendations by federal, state, and local officeholders, seeking clarification on whether any arrangement would constitute improper in-kind contributions under the slate mailer exemption.
